fields.icd_conflict_detail: All three documents (referral form, clinical notes, and prescription) consistently document ICD-10 code G12.21 (Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis). No conflict was found across any of the documents.
fields.priority_reason: Patient has a progressive and severe neurological condition (ALS, G12.21) requiring mobility assistance; ALS is a high-acuity diagnosis with rapidly declining functional status, warranting urgent equipment provision.
fields.notes: 1) Clinical notes reference a 'post-operative assessment' and 'wound healing within normal parameters,' which is clinically inconsistent with an ALS diagnosis — ALS is a progressive neurodegenerative disease and not typically associated with post-operative recovery; this may indicate a documentation error or template copy-paste issue and should be reviewed. 2) The referral lists the injury date as 07/10/2026, which is also atypical for ALS, a chronic progressive condition rather than an acute injury — the workers' compensation context should be verified for appropriateness. 3) Interpreter/language support in Tagalog should be arranged prior to delivery appointment. 4) Occupational therapy was recommended alongside DME provision per clinical notes — consider coordinating OT referral. 5) Prescription is valid through 10/24/2026 (90 days from 07/26/2026). 6) Expected duration of need is 6 months.
